raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's ports have experienced increased year-over-year levels of cargo volume.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has dramatically impacted the goods movement system in California and across the nation.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the fragility of the goods movement sector and the supply chain.",
    "The Ports of Los Angeles and Long Beach have been handling an unprecedented number of ships arriving at the same time.",
    "Disruptions in transportation and warehouse availability have slowed the offloading of containers and contributed to a backlog.",
    "Trucks have been temporarily allowed to carry heavier loads of up to 88,000 pounds on State Highway and Interstate routes between key ports in California.",
    "SB 1356 would have permanently allowed fuel haulers to carry heavier loads, reducing emissions and alleviating congestion on the road.",
    "Allowing fuel haulers to transport more fuel could aid firefighters battling wildfires in California.",
    "Certain vehicles transporting logs or cotton module movers have exemptions to exceed the maximum pound limit."
  ]
}
```
